summary: Rati went on a 4,600-line rampage through GSIP, overhauling DataProviders and cloud masking. He’s officially turned the repo into a library-ready beast that’s as lean on VRAM as it is mean on pixels.

Rati went big on GSIP today, dropping over 1,100 lines to launch a new DataProvider API. He’s effectively streamlined the pipeline, allowing programmatic data to bypass intermediate files and stream straight to the GPU.

Rati’s "Bloom" ecosystem just had a 3,800-line growth spurt; he scrapped Waybar for a custom eww island, built a GTK4 greeter from scratch, and overhauled the settings app into a powerhouse of desktop customization.

Rati kept it surgical today, refining bloom-iso and bloom-shell with just twelve precise lines. He smoothed out window behaviors and squashed shell bugs, proving that major UI fixes don’t always require massive codebases.
Rati dropped a hefty 800-line update to GSIP, teaching it to handle Sentinel-2 offsets and optimizing GPU memory so ZoR can dream big without choking your system's RAM.
Rati gave GSIP a surgical tune-up today, fixing tiling compatibility and making halos optional. With 78 lines of focused changes, he’s ensuring his spatial logic is as flexible as it is precise.
Rati buffed GSIP with 350+ lines today, introducing "Skip" and "Mirror" modes to handle data gaps like a pro while strictly enforcing a four-patch minimum. No pixel gets left behind on his watch!
